commit.message:
fix(shader): fix depth prepass transparency — void prepass fragment + AlphaMode::Mask

Root cause: the depth prepass was writing depth for ALL voxels including
transparent/discarded ones. Objects behind transparent voxels (e.g. the
player) had lower reverse-Z depth values and failed the GreaterEqual depth
test in the main pass, making them invisible (gray/black pixels).

Two-part fix:
1. Change AlphaMode::Opaque → AlphaMode::Mask(0.001) for dithered mode.
   This sets MeshPipelineKey::MAY_DISCARD, which causes Bevy to include the
   fragment stage in the depth prepass pipeline when a custom prepass shader
   is registered. The 0.001 cutoff never fires (base_color.a is always 1.0),
   so visual behaviour is identical to Opaque.

2. Fix occlusion_material_prepass.wgsl to use a void return type.
   For depth-only prepass, PREPASS_FRAGMENT is NOT defined, so FragmentOutput
   does not exist in bevy_pbr::prepass_io. The fragment function writes no
   outputs — depth is written automatically from the fragment position.
   Only discard logic (dither + region checks) is needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(shader): use minimal prepass shader to fix black occluded voxels

The full occlusion_material.wgsl calls pbr_input_from_standard_material()
which requires lighting uniforms and shadow maps that are not bound
during a depth-only prepass. Running it as prepass_fragment_shader()
produced garbage discard decisions, writing wrong depth into the depth
buffer. The main forward pass then blocked geometry behind transparent
voxels (player, floor) via the bad prepass depth, showing black holes.

Fix: add occlusion_material_prepass.wgsl — a minimal shader that
replicates only the uniform struct, dither helpers, and discard logic.
No PBR setup, no texture sampling. Depth is written automatically from
the fragment position after the surviving fragments are determined.

base_color.a is always 1.0 for voxel chunks so occlusion_alpha is used
directly, matching the effective final_alpha of the main pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(shader): separate deferred and depth prepass output branches

PREPASS_PIPELINE is set for both deferred GBuffer and forward depth
prepass pipelines. The previous code called deferred_output() for all
prepass variants, which wrote to unbound GBuffer textures when running
as a forward depth prepass, corrupting the main pass lighting output
(gray/black occluded voxels).

Fix: guard deferred_output() behind #ifdef DEFERRED_PREPASS. The depth
prepass path now declares an empty FragmentOutput — depth is written
automatically from the fragment position after all discard logic runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(contact): harden DNS mail failure scenario (#63)

Co-authored-by: QA Bot <qa-bot@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ws: make InputCallback fire-and-forget to unblock DataChannel handler

Wrap the gRPC SendInput call in a goroutine so the pion DataChannel
message handler returns immediately. Previously Player 2's callback
blocked behind Player 1's ~2-5ms gRPC round-trip. Now both players'
input is dispatched concurrently. Timeout reduced from 100ms to 50ms
since the goroutine no longer holds up the DataChannel pipeline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: cover contact mail timeout failure path (#62)

Adds deterministic SMTP timeout coverage using fake timers and transport-level mocking; verifies non-200 failure contract and no sensitive leak.

Co-authored-by: QA Bot <qa-bot@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(rate-limit): add connectTimeout and commandTimeout to prevent request handler stalls

ioredis defaults connectTimeout to 10 s and has no commandTimeout.
A Redis that accepts TCP but stops responding would block the contact
route handler for up to 10 s on connect, or indefinitely on eval.

Set connectTimeout=2000ms and commandTimeout=1000ms so a misbehaving
Redis fails fast and the existing catch blocks degrade to in-memory.

enableOfflineQueue=false was already set; these two options close the
remaining gap identified in the graceful-degradation checklist item.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(cd): remove CI self-role-assignment + document prerequisite

The CI service principal (Contributor) cannot assign RBAC roles to itself.
Remove the self-assignment logic from setup-tfstate.sh for CI and document
the one-time Storage Blob Data Contributor role assignment as a prerequisite
in the README.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(lint): replace any cast with typed Record to satisfy next lint

eslint-disable for @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any was invalid because
the project's ESLint config (next lint) does not include that rule.
Replace the cast with Record<string, unknown> which is type-safe and
avoids the rule reference entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: merge main into box/test-contact-api-baseline (resolve route + test conflicts)

Merge resolves divergence between the CSRF/CORS security test branch and the
changes that landed in main (#54 CI, #55 a11y, #60/#61 mail failure tests).

Resolution strategy:
- route.ts: kept OPTIONS handler + security/CORS headers (PR branch),
  incorporated sendContactMail integration (main)
- route.test.ts: kept all 35 CSRF/CORS/security tests (PR branch),
  added mail failure describe block using vi.spyOn pattern (main)
  Result: 38 tests, all passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add English translation of DEPLOYMENT.md

Translate the French deployment guide to English while preserving all
code blocks, commands, file paths, URLs, and technical terms.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(security): assert no GET handler on contact endpoint (CSRF exempt by omission)

Documents the intentional design: this endpoint has no GET export, so there is
no read-mutation surface for CSRF to target on GET.  The test catches accidental
GET handler additions that would open a new attack surface.

OWASP coverage: A01 (CSRF via method-omission), A05 (surface reduction).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(contact): harden mail failure coverage (#61)

Replace response-layer stubbing with module-level mail transport mocking and add deterministic timeout/rejection/throw failure-path assertions. Wire contact route through a dedicated sendContactMail boundary with explicit timeout handling so failures return safe 500 envelopes without hanging.

Co-authored-by: QA Bot <qa-bot@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(contact): cover mail transport failure scenarios

Add deterministic tests for timeout, rejection, and thrown network errors via a module-level mocked mail boundary. Wire contact route through sendContactMail with timeout so failure paths are testable and return safe generic 500 responses.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(rate-limit): add window-slide and expiry coverage

- in-memory: verify counter resets after window expires using fake timers
- Redis path: verify (now - window) pruning cutoff is passed so expired
  entries are evicted by ZREMRANGEBYSCORE in the Lua script
- Satisfies test requirement (d) from Moses's brief: old requests outside
  the window must not count

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix video height, add max players per ROM config

- CSS: add align-items: start to .play-body so video area
  respects aspect-ratio: 3/2 instead of stretching to sidebar height
- CSS: fix .play-video-area aspect-ratio to 3/2 (GBA native: 240x160)
- API: add PATCH /api/games/:id endpoint to update maxPlayers and description
- Store: add UpdateMaxPlayers() and UpdateDescription() to library.Store
- Frontend: add patchGame() API helper in api.ts

- Frontend: LobbyCreate player dropdown shows at least 1-4 options;
  ROM's maxPlayers is the pre-selected default

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
security: add defence-in-depth headers and credentialed CORS test coverage

route.ts:
- Add SECURITY_HEADERS constant: X-Frame-Options: DENY,
  X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Cache-Control: no-store
- Add withSecurityHeaders() helper applied to every exit path in
  both OPTIONS and POST (204, 403, 415, 429, 400, 422, 200, 500)
- X-Frame-Options/nosniff guard against legacy browser frame/MIME
  attack surface; Cache-Control prevents proxy caching of API responses

route.test.ts:
- Add credentialed cross-origin POST test: Cookie header present,
  allowlisted origin, asserts ACAO echoed + ACAC: true + no wildcard
- Add 'security response headers' describe block (8 tests) asserting
  X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff,
  Cache-Control: no-store on success, error, CSRF-403, and OPTIONS

Total: 34 tests pass (25 base + 9 new). No regressions.

OWASP: A05 (security misconfiguration / missing security headers)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test: cover contact mail failure scenarios (#60)

Add deterministic timeout, SMTP rejection, and network mid-send failure tests for the contact endpoint. Assert 500 generic responses, explicit error logging, and non-crashing behavior via resolved response promises.

Co-authored-by: QA Bot <qa-bot@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(rate-limit): true sliding window via Redis sorted set + Redis mock tests

- Replace fixed-window INCR+PEXPIRE Lua script with a sliding-window
  sorted-set implementation (ZADD/ZREMRANGEBYSCORE/ZCARD) to eliminate
  the 2x MAX_REQUESTS boundary-burst vulnerability.
- Each request gets a unique UUID member so ZADD never clobbers an
  existing entry when two requests arrive in the same millisecond.
- All four Lua operations (evict, count, insert, expire) are wrapped in
  a single EVAL call — fully atomic on the Redis server.
- Add explicit FAIL-OPEN documentation: Redis unavailability degrades to
  in-memory counter rather than blocking all requests (contact form is
  not safety-critical; in-memory still enforces limit per-process).
- Add lib/rate-limit.test.ts (12 tests) that stub ioredis via vi.mock:
    • In-memory fallback: allow/limit/per-IP isolation tests
    • Redis path: verifies Lua script contains ZREMRANGEBYSCORE/ZADD/ZCARD,
      checks ARGV layout (timestamp, window, max, UUID), allow/block on
      eval return 0/1, unique-member-per-request assertion
    • Fail-open: connect-throw and eval-throw both degrade gracefully

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(cd): consolidate state bootstrap into preprovision hook

- Remove duplicate Bootstrap step from cd.yml (preprovision hook handles it)
- Update setup-tfstate.sh to detect CI vs local identity:
  - CI: uses ARM_CLIENT_ID to look up service principal
  - Local: uses az ad signed-in-user for current user
- Add 30s role propagation delay after assignment

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(rendering): configurable shadow quality setting

- Add ShadowQuality enum (None/CharactersOnly/Low/High, default=Low)
  to OcclusionConfig replacing the unused hide_shadows stub field
- Add shadow_params_for_quality() helper: maps quality level to
  (shadows_enabled, CascadeShadowConfig) — single source of truth
- spawn_lighting() now reads OcclusionConfig to apply quality at spawn
- ChunkSpawnContext gains shadow_quality field; spawn_voxels_chunked
  inserts NotShadowCaster on VoxelChunk entities for CharactersOnly
- New apply_shadow_quality_system (Visual set, InGame|Paused):
  change-detected on OcclusionConfig + Added<VoxelChunk>;
  updates DirectionalLight, CascadeShadowConfig, NotShadowCaster at runtime
- Settings menu: HideShadows row replaced by ShadowQuality (Off/Characters/Low/High)
- settings.ron: hide_shadows → shadow_quality: Low
- docs/developer-guide/architecture.md: Shadow Quality section added
- 5 new tests in shadow_quality.rs (all pass)

Default Low cuts p95 frame spikes from ~38ms to <15ms by reducing
shadow cascade volume from 4×(100u)³ to 2×(20u)³.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add carpi deployment notes and broker hostname fix

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

so it fills the video area container properly.

feat(diagnostics): add frame_interval_us and frame_cpu_us measurements

- frame_interval_us: wall-clock time between frame starts (First→First)
  This is what determines FPS (includes GPU, vsync, idle wait).
- frame_cpu_us: time from First to Last each frame
  Captures all main-thread work (game logic + Bevy systems + render extract).
- frame_interval_us − frame_cpu_us ≈ render pipeline overhead

Both are lock-free using AtomicU64 nanos since created_at.
Data appears as new label columns in the benchmark CSV.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
security: complete CORS response headers and OPTIONS preflight handler

- Add buildCorsHeaders() helper returning Access-Control-Allow-Origin,
  Access-Control-Allow-Credentials, Allow-Methods, Allow-Headers, Max-Age
- Add withCors() helper to attach CORS headers to allowed-origin responses
- Add OPTIONS export for CORS preflight: 204 for allowed origins, 403 otherwise
- POST responses from allowed origins carry full CORS headers so cross-origin
  callers can read structured error bodies (415/429/422/400/500)
- 403 CSRF rejections carry NO CORS headers — attacker JS cannot read the body
- Update middleware order comment to document OPTIONS/preflight ordering
  rationale (OPTIONS runs before CSRF; preflight carries no user data)

Tests (25 pass, up from 20):
- POST allowed origin -> Access-Control-Allow-Origin echoed, not wildcard
- POST allowed origin -> Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true
- POST disallowed origin (403) -> no Access-Control-Allow-Origin header
- OPTIONS allowed origin -> 204 + full CORS headers (preflight handshake)
- OPTIONS disallowed origin -> 403, no CORS headers
- All 20 prior tests continue to pass

OWASP: A01 (CSRF), A05 (security misconfiguration/header leakage)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

security: complete CORS response headers and OPTIONS preflight handler

- Add buildCorsHeaders() helper returning Access-Control-Allow-Origin,
  Access-Control-Allow-Credentials, Allow-Methods, Allow-Headers, Max-Age
- Add withCors() helper to attach CORS headers to allowed-origin responses
- Add OPTIONS export for CORS preflight (204 for allowed, 403 for others)
- POST responses from allowed origins now carry full CORS headers so
  cross-origin callers can read structured error bodies (415/429/422/400/500)
- 403 CSRF rejections intentionally carry NO CORS headers to prevent
  attacker JS from reading the rejection body
- Update middleware order comment to document OPTIONS/preflight ordering
  rationale (OPTIONS runs before CSRF — preflight carries no user data)

Tests (25 pass):
- POST allowed origin -> Access-Control-Allow-Origin echoed, not wildcard
- POST allowed origin -> Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true
- POST disallowed origin (403) -> no Access-Control-Allow-Origin header
- OPTIONS allowed origin -> 204 + full CORS headers (preflight handshake)
- OPTIONS disallowed origin -> 403, no CORS headers
- All 20 existing tests continue to pass

OWASP: A01 (CSRF), A05 (security misconfiguration/header leakage)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(diagnostics): frame profiler for debug builds

Adds a FrameProfilerPlugin that records per-system CPU timings to a
CSV file in target/benchmarks/profile_<timestamp>.csv. Only active
in debug builds (cfg(debug_assertions)) — release builds are
unaffected.

Architecture:
- FrameProfiler resource: SyncSender<ProfileLine> + AtomicU64 frame counter
- Background writer thread: bounded channel (8192 slots) + BufWriter<File>
- ProfileScope RAII guard: writes frame,label,duration_us row on drop
- try_send() used in drop: never blocks the game thread

Instrumented systems:
  move_player, apply_gravity, apply_physics (per frame)
  detect_interior_system, update_occlusion_uniforms (per frame, throttled)
  update_chunk_lods, follow_player_camera (per frame)
  spawn_map_system + spawn_voxels_chunked (map load, one-time)

Usage after running the game:
  # Average µs per system, sorted slowest first
  awk -F',' 'NR>1 {s[$2]+=$3;c[$2]++} END {for(l in s) print s[l]/c[l], l}'     target/benchmarks/profile_*.csv | sort -rn

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(rate-limit): atomic INCR+PEXPIRE via Lua eval; correct algorithm label

- Replace two-step INCR + PEXPIRE with a single Redis EVAL Lua script.
  The previous code had a race: if INCR returned 1 but PEXPIRE then threw,
  the key would have no TTL, causing count to grow unbounded and
  permanently blocking the IP (or leaking Redis memory indefinitely).
  The Lua script runs atomically on the Redis server so the race is gone.

- Correct the algorithm label from 'sliding window' to 'fixed window'.
  INCR+PEXPIRE-anchored-to-first-request is a fixed-window algorithm.
  Added a note about the 2×MAX_REQUESTS boundary-burst behavior so
  reviewers understand the trade-off explicitly.

- RedisLikeClient type: replaced incr/pexpire with eval (the only method
  now needed from the client for rate-limit operations).

All 23 tests pass (in-memory fallback path exercised by test suite).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(cd): use use_microsoft_entra_id in backend config for Azure AD auth

The ARM_USE_AZUREAD env var doesn't propagate through azd's subprocess.
Place auth config directly in provider.conf.json instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(build): remove stale Voxel import/spawn after revert

The revert of 'add NotShadowCaster' restored the Voxel import and
spawn call in chunks.rs, but the Voxel struct was already removed by
the orphaned-entity cleanup commit. Remove the now-dangling references.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(cd): switch to Azure AD auth for state backend + add preprovision hook

- Replace ARM_ACCESS_KEY with ARM_USE_AZUREAD (key auth blocked by policy)
- Auto-assign Storage Blob Data Contributor role on state storage account
- Add preprovision hook in azure.yaml so setup-tfstate.sh runs automatically
- Simplify README: azd up now handles everything via hook

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(ecs): remove orphaned Voxel marker entities

A Voxel marker entity was spawned for every voxel in the map via
ctx.commands.spawn(Voxel) in spawn_voxels_chunked(). No system ever
queried or used these entities. They accumulated in the ECS world
proportional to map size, consuming memory and slowing hot-reload
despawn/respawn cycles.

Remove the spawn call, the Voxel import in chunks.rs, and the Voxel
component struct from components.rs.

Docs: docs/bugs/fix-orphaned-voxel-entities/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(chunks): add NotShadowCaster to VoxelChunk entities

All VoxelChunk mesh entities were participating in 4 cascaded shadow
depth passes every frame (DirectionalLight, shadows_enabled: true).
Shadow depth rendering uses the light frustum, not the main camera
frustum, so off-screen chunks were still rendered into shadow maps
every frame. GPU cost scaled with total map voxel count regardless
of camera direction.

Add NotShadowCaster to both ChunkMaterial::Occlusion and
ChunkMaterial::Standard spawn paths. Main-camera frustum culling
via Aabb is unaffected.

Docs: docs/bugs/fix-chunk-shadow-casting/
Investigation: docs/investigations/2026-03-16-1310-off-screen-voxel-performance.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Update Azure/setup-azd@v1.0.0 to @v2 (retired CDN fix)
- Rename Terraform outputs to SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE for azd compatibility
- Add backend 'azurerm' block in main.tf for remote state
- Add provider.conf.json template for azd backend config substitution
- Add setup-tfstate.sh for local dev bootstrap (creates storage + sets azd env)
- CD workflow bootstraps state storage via Azure CLI before azd up
- Auth via ARM_ACCESS_KEY (storage account key) for both local and CI
- Update README with Azure deployment prerequisites

docs: add documentation for build-deploy-windows-services workflow

fix(occlusion): gate systems to InGame and Paused states only

detect_interior_system, update_occlusion_uniforms, and debug_draw_occlusion_zone
were registered in Update without a run_if condition, causing the scheduler to
dispatch them in every GameState (IntroAnimation, TitleScreen, LoadingMap,
Settings). Added .run_if(in_state(GameState::InGame).or(in_state(GameState::Paused)))
to the system chain in OcclusionPlugin::build(), consistent with all other
gameplay systems in main.rs.

Paused is included so the occlusion visual is preserved while the pause menu
is overlaid on the game world.

commit.message:
perf(shader): add XZ squared-distance early-exit in calculate_occlusion_alpha

Fragments above the height threshold but far from the player in XZ were
reaching normalize() and point_to_ray_distance_xz() (both involve sqrt)
before the radius early-exit fired. The new check computes squared XZ
distance from the player using dot() only — no sqrt — and returns 1.0
when the fragment exceeds (occlusion_radius × 2.0)² from the player.

Execution order is now:
  ① height check (unchanged) — covers floor/lower-wall fragments
  ② XZ squared-distance check (new) — covers distant horizontal fragments
  ③ normalize + ray distance + smoothstep — near-player fragments only

XZ_MARGIN_FACTOR = 2.0 const added at module scope after BAYER_MATRIX.
Change is confined to assets/shaders/occlusion_material.wgsl.
Planning docs added under docs/bugs/fix-occlusion-shader-early-exit/.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: replace nonlocal flag with sentinel exception in datetime_prefix

Instead of setting a nonlocal boolean from the re.sub callback to
signal an invalid ordinal, raise a private _InvalidOrdinalError and
catch it at the call site. This makes the control flow explicit and
removes the side-channel mutation.

refactor: use module-level loggers via getLogger(__name__)

Replace repeated per-function logging.getLogger("normfn") calls with a
single module-level logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) in each module
(core, dates, files, __main__). Child loggers propagate to the normfn
root logger configured in __main__.py, so behaviour is unchanged.

In __main__.py the setup call keeps targeting getLogger("normfn")
explicitly (renamed pkg_logger) while the module-level logger handles
the FatalError logging.

fix(hot-reload): use Option<Res<SpatialGrid>> in move_player and apply_physics

SpatialGrid is removed by handle_map_reload before respawn completes.
Both systems now accept Option<Res<SpatialGrid>> and return early when
the resource is absent, preventing the ECS panic on the transition frame.

- Added `rebuild_pending: bool` field to `InteriorState` (default false)
- `detect_interior_system`: when `Added<SubVoxel>` or `RemovedComponents<SubVoxel>`
  is non-empty, set `rebuild_pending = true` and return early — no O(200k)
  rebuild during the hot-reload respawn frame
- On the first settle frame (both change queries empty, `rebuild_pending` true),
  clear the flag, reset the cache, and rebuild exactly once; detection resumes
  on the same frame
- Cold-start path (cache None, no spawn in progress) is unchanged
- Added 4 new unit tests covering flag transitions: deferred while spawning,
  fires on settle, cold start builds immediately, flag stays clear after rebuild
- Updated `docs/developer-guide/architecture.md` interior detection section
  with the new defer pattern and added a coding rule

commit.message:
perf(collision): pre-fetch spatial grid query in move_player and share with apply_physics

- Added `PreFetchedCollisionEntities` resource to `resources.rs` to hold a
  frame-level slice of nearby sub-voxel entities and the AABB bounds used to
  fetch them
- Registered resource with `init_resource` in `main.rs`
- `move_player` (Movement set): computes one widened AABB before movement
  checks (expanded by |move_delta| horizontally, +SUB_VOXEL_SIZE +
  STEP_UP_TOLERANCE upward) and writes entities + bounds to the resource;
  all axis checks and step-up re-checks reuse this cached slice instead of
  re-querying the grid
- `apply_physics` (Physics set): reads the cache; uses it when the player's
  physics AABB is fully contained within the cached bounds; falls back to its
  own `SpatialGrid` query otherwise
- `check_sub_voxel_collision`: added `prefetched: Option<&[Entity]>` param so
  callers that already hold a slice bypass the grid entirely
- Made `SUB_VOXEL_SIZE` and `STEP_UP_TOLERANCE` `pub(super)` so
  `player_movement.rs` can reference them for AABB widening
- Added 5 new unit tests covering: empty-slice skip, None-fallback query,
  widened AABB geometry, cache bounds containment, and empty-cache grid fallback
- Updated `docs/developer-guide/architecture.md` with `PreFetchedCollisionEntities`
  resource entry and pre-fetched collision cache section
- Added planning docs under `docs/bugs/fix-spatial-grid-multiple-lookups/`

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(frontend): add encodeErc20Approve method (#11959)

# Motivation

When staking/unstaking ERC4626 vaults, the fee estimation becomes a bit
more complex. One of the steps is to get the gas needed for ERC20
approval (without transfer). The `encodeErc20Approve` is gonna prepare
the object that is going to be used in `safeGasEstimate`.

commit.message:
Legg til InntektskomponentV2Klient med sammenligning mot V1 (#644)

### **Behov / Bakgrunn**

Migrering til V2 av inntektskomponent-API-et, tilsvarende
[fp-inntektsmelding#128](https://github.com/navikt/fp-inntektsmelding/pull/128).
Kjører V1 og V2 parallelt og sammenligner resultater før full overgang.

### **Løsning**
Ny restklient for InntektskomponentV2, tilsvarende som i foreldrepenger.
I første omgang vil vi kun sammenligne resultatet med v1.


```java
// GrunnlagTjeneste – sammenligner stille i bakgrunnen
var inntektsopplysninger = inntektTjeneste.hentInntekt(...);
try {
    var iV2 = inntektTjeneste.hentInntektV2(...);
    if (!Inntektsopplysninger.erLik(inntektsopplysninger, iV2)) {
        LOG.info("InntektV2 diff for {}", uuid);
    }
} catch (Exception e) {
    LOG.info("InntektV2 feil for {}", uuid, e);
}
```


<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->


<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> I k9-inntektsmelding trenger vi å gå over til
InntektskomponentV2Klient. Tilsvarende har blitt gjort i
fp-inntektsmelding i denne pren:
https://github.com/navikt/fp-inntektsmelding/pull/128. Jeg ønsker å få
en tilsvarende PR i k9-inntektsmelding repoet. Jeg ønsker at vi går mot
både V2 og V1 og sammenligner slik som det ble gjort i
fp-inntektsmelding


</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

Created from [VS
Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/copilot/copilot-coding-agent).

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

you](https://github.com/navikt/k9-inntektsmelding/issues/new?title=✨+Set+up+Copilot+instructions&body=Configure%20instructions%20for%20this%20repository%20as%20documented%20in%20%5BBest%20practices%20for%20Copilot%20coding%20agent%20in%20your%20repository%5D%28https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips%29%2E%0A%0A%3COnboard%20this%20repo%3E&assignees=copilot)
— coding agent works faster and does higher quality work when set up for

commit.message:
Add Azure-scoped security feeds: Sentinel, Defender for Cloud, Azure ATP

Add Microsoft Sentinel, Microsoft Defender for Cloud, and Azure Advanced
Threat Protection blogs to the Tech Community feed sources. These are
Azure-native security services that fit the scope of the feed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(security): CSRF/CORS behavioral tests and middleware order audit

- Document [1]-[6] guard execution order in route.ts with rationale:
  origin check → content-type → rate-limit → parse → sanitise → validate
- Explain origin-header CSRF mechanism (no separate token) and why
  CSRF check runs before rate-limit (cross-origin probes must not burn
  rate-limit budget for legitimate users)
- Add 'CSRF / origin guard — behavioral' describe block (4 tests):
  * allows same-origin requests (no Origin header)
  * allows matching configured origin → HTTP 200
  * rejects disallowed origin → HTTP 403, structured body, no sensitive data
  * rejects null Origin (file:// CSRF attempt) → HTTP 403
- Add 'response header contracts' describe block (4 tests):
  * Content-Type: application/json on success, validation error, parse error
  * no Access-Control-Allow-Origin wildcard on cross-origin rejections
- Add 'error response body safety' describe block (2 tests):
  * no stack traces / node_modules / SyntaxError in validation errors
  * no stack traces / node_modules / SyntaxError in parse errors
- All 20 tests pass; CSRF/origin tests conditional on HAS_CSRF flag so
  the file runs safely against both baseline and hardened PR #53 code

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(rate-limit): replace in-memory Map with Redis-backed implementation

- Extract rate limiter into lib/rate-limit.ts with environment-aware backend
- Uses Redis (ioredis) when REDIS_URL or REDIS_HOST is set, enabling shared
  state across multiple server instances
- Falls back transparently to the original in-memory sliding-window Map when
  neither env var is set — local dev, CI, and single-instance deploys are
  unaffected with no Redis dependency required
- Redis client is lazy-init with error recovery: a connection failure or
  mid-request Redis error degrades gracefully to in-memory without throwing
- route.ts: replace inline rate-limit code with import of async isRateLimited
  (call site updated to await; window/max constants unchanged)
- package.json: add ioredis ^5.10.0 as a runtime dependency
- .env.local.example: document REDIS_URL and REDIS_HOST as optional variables

All 10 contact API tests pass with the in-memory fallback active.

fix(lod): throttle update_chunk_lods with distance guard and LodConfig

- Add LOD_MOVEMENT_THRESHOLD const (0.5 world units) near LOD_DISTANCES
- Add Local<Vec3> last_camera_pos + distance guard to update_chunk_lods;
  stationary camera now costs O(1) instead of O(N)
- Add Added<VoxelChunk> bypass so newly spawned chunks always get correct LOD
- Add LodConfig resource (movement_threshold: f32) for runtime tuning;
  registered via app.init_resource::<LodConfig>() in main.rs
- Export LodConfig and LOD_MOVEMENT_THRESHOLD from map mod
- Add 6 unit tests covering guard logic and LodConfig default
- Update docs/developer-guide/architecture.md with LOD throttle section (v2.4.0)
- Add planning docs: requirements.md, architecture.md, ticket.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix PR unresolved comment badges

Render unresolved-count badges on PR titles, count unresolved threads (1 per thread), and update API/UI tests.

fix: add .npmrc with legacy-peer-deps for Vercel build

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

🧙‍♀️ Hogwarts Math Academy - Initial Release

Harry Potter-themed Hebrew (RTL) math practice app for kids.
- 6 Hogwarts year levels: doubles → near-doubles strategy
- Full Hebrew RTL with LTR math expression isolation
- Synthesized magical sound effects (Web Audio API)
- Hash-based navigation with browser back/forward support
- Session persistence with mid-practice save/resume
- Schema-versioned localStorage with cross-tab sync
- SVG character illustrations (Harry, Hermione, Ron, Dumbledore, Hedwig)
- Framer Motion animations throughout
- 27 passing tests (RTL, game engine, near-doubles, Hebrew QA)

Stack: React 19 + Vite 8 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS v4

fix(camera): use frame-rate-independent exponential decay for lerp/slerp

- Replace follow_speed * delta with 1 - exp(-follow_speed * delta) in follow_player_camera
- Replace rotation_speed * delta with 1 - exp(-rotation_speed * delta) in rotate_camera
- Raise follow_speed spawn default from 5.0 to 15.0 for responsive third-person feel
- Extract lerp_alpha() helper and add 4 unit tests confirming frame-rate independence
- Update docs/developer-guide/architecture.md with camera lerp section (v2.3.0)
- Add planning docs: requirements.md, architecture.md, ticket.md

fix(occlusion): restore Hybrid as default OcclusionMode

Revert the ShaderBased default introduced in the previous commit.
OcclusionMode::Hybrid remains the default; only region_update_interval
change (10→60) and event-based cache invalidation are kept.

Update requirements, ticket, and architecture docs to reflect the
corrected scope.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(occlusion): eliminate interior detection BFS frame spikes

- Raise OcclusionConfig.region_update_interval default 10 → 60 (BFS runs
  at most once per second at 60 fps, down from 6 times per second)
- Change OcclusionConfig.mode default Hybrid → ShaderBased (BFS path
  skipped entirely for the common case; Hybrid/RegionBased remain available)
- Remove InteriorState.cache_entity_count field; replace entity-count
  scan (O(n) every detection cycle) with Bevy Added<SubVoxel> +
  RemovedComponents<SubVoxel> event-based cache invalidation
- Occupancy HashSet now rebuilt only when SubVoxel entities actually change
- Add 6 unit tests: config defaults, cache reuse, cache invalidation on
  add/remove, pre-existing ceiling detection tests preserved
- Update docs/developer-guide/architecture.md with new cache-invalidation
  pattern and interior detection performance section (v2.2.0)
- Resolve open question: interior_height_threshold default deferred
